revision.comment: Created page with "Mariobanana has repeatedly ignored my many good faith gestures in favor of perpetuating a narrative against me # I have acknowledged the "scattering" problem and have been tyring to fix it. This is why I made [[Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Shinto]] and consolidated many threads into it. So there can be a unified place for discussion # As you can see in the diff here https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Religions&oldid=2437865735 it did not i..."
revision.text:
Mariobanana has repeatedly ignored my many good faith gestures in favor of perpetuating a narrative against me
# I have acknowledged the "scattering" problem and have been tyring to fix it. This is why I made [[Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Shinto]] and consolidated many threads into it. So there can be a unified place for discussion # As you can see in the diff here https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Religions&oldid=2437865735 it did not in fact archive his post, at least if he meant the current discussion. This thread [[Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Religions#Proposed_automatic_edits]] was archived, since it is consolidated here [[Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Shinto#Proposed_automatic_edits]]. In fact I actively saw that he was trying to respond in the part scheduled to archive, and I edited the conversation to ensure that his comment was not archived, and that he could continue the discussion there https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk%3AWikiProject_Religions&diff=2437533525&oldid=2437527078 # After [https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk%3AWikiProject_Religions&diff=2435926913&oldid=2435925451 he commented opposition to the bot], I [https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Religions&diff=next&oldid=2435926913 immediately stopped the bot] and [https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Religions&diff=next&oldid=2435927740 offered to revert the edits]. He never responded or acknowledged any of this, and I dediced to revert all the edits as a sign of good faith. # While he never actually asked me to avoid mass merges, I decided on a simpler less risky solution based on his edits on this page # The edits on {{q|Q11397991}} were a misunderstanding. The edit summaries make it clear. # MarioBanana directy contributed to the scattering by demanding first a move of the discussion off of this page,
What I posted in English is the original but I will post the Japanese too for users who prefer Japanese. But keep in mind the English is the original.

Mariobanana has repeatedly ignored my many good faith gestures in favor of perpetuating a narrative against me
# I have acknowledged the "scattering" problem and have been tyring to fix it. This is why I made [[Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Shinto]] and consolidated many threads into it. So there can be a unified place for discussion # As you can see in the diff here https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Religions&oldid=2437865735 it did not in fact archive his post, at least if he meant the current discussion. This thread [[Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Religions#Proposed_automatic_edits]] was archived, since it is consolidated here [[Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Shinto#Proposed_automatic_edits]]. In fact I actively saw that he was trying to respond in the part scheduled to archive, and I edited the conversation to ensure that his comment was not archived, and that he could continue the discussion there https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk%3AWikiProject_Religions&diff=2437533525&oldid=2437527078 # After [https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk%3AWikiProject_Religions&diff=2435926913&oldid=2435925451 he commented opposition to the bot], I [https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Religions&diff=next&oldid=2435926913 immediately stopped the bot] and [https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Religions&diff=next&oldid=2435927740 offered to revert the edits]. He never responded or acknowledged any of this, and I dediced to revert all the edits as a sign of good faith. # While he never actually asked me to avoid mass merges, I decided on a simpler less risky solution based on his edits on this page # The edits on {{q|Q11397991}} were a misunderstanding. The edit summaries make it clear. Because of the large amount of information that was removed, I undid with a comment that I thought it was a mistake, since it was one of the few things undid not added by me
=== Further points against Mariobanana ===
# Contributed to the scattering ## ip user made the conversations unreadable due to his request https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Religions&direction=next&oldid=2427722986 ## Demanded the thread be moved away from this thread https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata%3A%E4%BA%95%E6%88%B8%E7%AB%AF&diff=2424151987&oldid=2424118803
What I posted in English is the original but I will post the Japanese too for users who prefer Japanese. But keep in mind the English is the original.

Mariobanana has repeatedly ignored my many good faith gestures in favor of perpetuating a narrative against me
# I have acknowledged the "scattering" problem and have been tyring to fix it. This is why I made [[Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Shinto]] and consolidated many threads into it. So there can be a unified place for discussion # As you can see in the diff here https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Religions&oldid=2437865735 it did not in fact archive his post, at least if he meant the current discussion. This thread [[Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Religions#Proposed_automatic_edits]] was archived, since it is consolidated here [[Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Shinto#Proposed_automatic_edits]]. In fact I actively saw that he was trying to respond in the part scheduled to archive, and I edited the conversation to ensure that his comment was not archived, and that he could continue the discussion there https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk%3AWikiProject_Religions&diff=2437533525&oldid=2437527078 # After [https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk%3AWikiProject_Religions&diff=2435926913&oldid=2435925451 he commented opposition to the bot], I [https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Religions&diff=next&oldid=2435926913 immediately stopped the bot] and [https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Religions&diff=next&oldid=2435927740 offered to revert the edits]. He never responded or acknowledged any of this, and I dediced to revert all the edits as a sign of good faith. # While he never actually asked me to avoid mass merges, I decided on a simpler less risky solution based on his edits on this page # The edits on {{q|Q11397991}} were a misunderstanding. The edit summaries make it clear. Because of the large amount of information that was removed, I undid with a comment that I thought it was a mistake, since it was one of the few things undid not added by me
=== Further points against Mariobanana ===
# Contributed to the scattering ## ip user made the conversations unreadable due to his request https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Religions&direction=next&oldid=2427722986 ## Demanded the thread be moved away from this thread https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata%3A%E4%BA%95%E6%88%B8%E7%AB%AF&diff=2424151987&oldid=2424118803 #He has criticized me for continuing editing without addressing old messages. But I am not sure what that even means. What level of consensus he wants for even basic things. What constitutes addressing a message that happened 3 or 4 months ago.
What I posted in English is the original but I will post the Japanese too for users who prefer Japanese. But keep in mind the English is the original.

Mariobanana has repeatedly ignored my many good faith gestures in favor of perpetuating a narrative against me
# '''I have acknowledged the "scattering" problem and have been tyring to fix it. This is why I made [[Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Shinto]] and consolidated many threads into it. So there can be a unified place for discussion''' # '''As you can see in the diff here https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Religions&oldid=2437865735 it did not in fact archive his post, at least if he meant the current discussion. This thread [[Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Religions#Proposed_automatic_edits]] was archived, since it is consolidated here [[Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Shinto#Proposed_automatic_edits]]. In fact I actively saw that he was trying to respond in the part scheduled to archive, and I edited the conversation to ensure that his comment was not archived, and that he could continue the discussion there https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk%3AWikiProject_Religions&diff=2437533525&oldid=2437527078''' # After [https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk%3AWikiProject_Religions&diff=2435926913&oldid=2435925451 he commented opposition to the bot], I [https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Religions&diff=next&oldid=2435926913 immediately stopped the bot] and [https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Religions&diff=next&oldid=2435927740 offered to revert the edits]. He never responded or acknowledged any of this, and I dediced to revert all the edits as a sign of good faith. # While he never actually asked me to avoid mass merges, I decided on a simpler less risky solution based on his edits on this page # The edits on {{q|Q11397991}} were a misunderstanding. The edit summaries make it clear. Because of the large amount of information that was removed, I undid with a comment that I thought it was a mistake, since it was one of the few things undid not added by me
=== Further points against Mariobanana ===
# '''Contributed to the scattering''' ## '''ip user made the conversations unreadable due to his request https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Religions&direction=next&oldid=2427722986''' ## '''Demanded the thread be moved away from this thread https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata%3A%E4%BA%95%E6%88%B8%E7%AB%AF&diff=2424151987&oldid=2424118803''' #He has criticized me for continuing editing without addressing old messages. But I am not sure what that even means. What level of consensus he wants for even basic things. What constitutes addressing a message that happened 3 or 4 months ago.
What I posted in English is the original but I will post the Japanese too for users who prefer Japanese. But keep in mind the English is the original.

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Immanuelle%2Fmariobanana_response here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24796114356|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!-- History offloaded: due to miraheze stability concerns, the edit history of this page has been offloaded to the XML archive. See https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Immanuelle%2Fmariobanana_response for full history. Last refreshed: [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24796114356|commit triggered pipeline]] --> Mariobanana has repeatedly ignored my many good faith gestures in favor of perpetuating a narrative against me
# '''I have acknowledged the "scattering" problem and have been tyring to fix it. This is why I made [[Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Shinto]] and consolidated many threads into it. So there can be a unified place for discussion''' # '''As you can see in the diff here https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Religions&oldid=2437865735 it did not in fact archive his post, at least if he meant the current discussion. This thread [[Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Religions#Proposed_automatic_edits]] was archived, since it is consolidated here [[Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Shinto#Proposed_automatic_edits]]. In fact I actively saw that he was trying to respond in the part scheduled to archive, and I edited the conversation to ensure that his comment was not archived, and that he could continue the discussion there https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk%3AWikiProject_Religions&diff=2437533525&oldid=2437527078''' # After [https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk%3AWikiProject_Religions&diff=2435926913&oldid=2435925451 he commented opposition to the bot], I [https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Religions&diff=next&oldid=2435926913 immediately stopped the bot] and [https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Religions&diff=next&oldid=2435927740 offered to revert the edits]. He never responded or acknowledged any of this, and I dediced to revert all the edits as a sign of good faith. # While he never actually asked me to avoid mass merges, I decided on a simpler less risky solution based on his edits on this page # The edits on {{q|Q11397991}} were a misunderstanding. The edit summaries make it clear. Because of the large amount of information that was removed, I undid with a comment that I thought it was a mistake, since it was one of the few things undid not added by me
=== Further points against Mariobanana ===
# '''Contributed to the scattering''' ## '''ip user made the conversations unreadable due to his request https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata_talk:WikiProject_Religions&direction=next&oldid=2427722986''' ## '''Demanded the thread be moved away from this thread https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata%3A%E4%BA%95%E6%88%B8%E7%AB%AF&diff=2424151987&oldid=2424118803''' #He has criticized me for continuing editing without addressing old messages. But I am not sure what that even means. What level of consensus he wants for even basic things. What constitutes addressing a message that happened 3 or 4 months ago.
What I posted in English is the original but I will post the Japanese too for users who prefer Japanese. But keep in mind the English is the original.
